title: COLCHICINE
description: Colchicine is a potent anti-inflammatory alkaloid derived from the Colchicum autumnale plant that primarily works by inhibiting microtubule polymerization and the NLRP3 inflammasome. It effectively reduces the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines like IL-1β and is standard therapy for gout, familial Mediterranean fever, and pericarditis.

research.title: Colchicine --- update on mechanisms of action and therapeutic uses
research.summary: This review details how colchicine inhibits neutrophil chemotaxis and the NLRP3 inflammasome, providing a broad mechanism for treating various systemic inflammatory conditions.

research.title: Cardiovascular effects and safety of long-term colchicine treatment: Cochrane review and meta-analysis
research.summary: A meta-analysis of 39 trials found that while colchicine did not affect all-cause mortality, it significantly reduced cardiovascular mortality in several analytical models.

sentiment.summary: Users view colchicine as a powerful medical tool for acute pain but express high caution regarding its narrow therapeutic index and dangerous drug interactions.

effects.label: Acute Gout:
effects.description: Highly effective when taken at the very first sign of a flare-up

effects.label: Cardiovascular Protection:
effects.description: Shows promise in reducing risk for patients with high cardiovascular inflammation

effects.label: Therapeutic Window:
effects.description: Effectiveness is limited by a very narrow range between a healing dose and a toxic dose

dosage.label: Standard Gout Flare:
dosage.description: Often initiated at 1.2mg followed by 0.6mg an hour later

dosage.label: Chronic Prophylaxis:
dosage.description: Low doses such as 0.5mg to 0.6mg daily are used to prevent inflammatory recurrence

dosage.label: Lethal Limit:
dosage.description: Noted as lethal at doses of approximately 1 mg/kg of body weight

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Distress:
sideEffects.description: Common reports of diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Dangerous interactions with CYP3A4 inhibitors like Quercetin or Grapefruit

sideEffects.label: Toxicity:
sideEffects.description: Overdose can lead to multi-organ failure due to its systemic effect on cell division

availability.label: Prescription Status:
availability.description: Generally regulated as a prescription medication rather than an over-the-counter supplement

availability.label: Sourcing Caution:
availability.description: Should not be confused with botanical extracts due to its high toxicity profile
